TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-22-2014 IN DEPT 318, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE SHOULD NOT ISSUE AS REQUESTED BY PETITIONER IN THE FIRST AMENDED WRIT OF PETITION FOR A WRIT OF MANDATE IS ARGUED AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON AUG-22-2014 BY JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER. THE COURT DISCLOSES THE FOLLOWING: JUDGE MILLER'S HUSBAND IS A GRADUATE OF HASTINGS LAW SCHOOL AND HAS MADE ALUMNI GIFTS TO THE HASTINGS ANNUAL FUND, INCLUDING RECENTLY A GIFT FOR HIS 35TH YEAR REUNION. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THERE ARE NO QUESTIONS OR OBJECTIONS FROM COUNSEL. THE COURT FURTHER DISCLOSES THE FOLLOWING: MS. WU IS A FORMER COLLEAGUE FROM THE LAW FIRM OF MORRISON & FOERSTER MANY YEARS AGO. THE COURT FINDS THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THERE ARE NO QUESTIONS OR OBJECTIONS FROM COUNSEL. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; REPORTER: RAMONA REINHARDT, CSR NO. 2677 [ATKINSON BAKER, 415.421.3021].

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING WRIT OF MANDATE IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOTION IS SET TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 318 AT 10:00 A.M. ON AUGUST 22, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF CCSF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF AMENDED JUDGMENT AFTER REMITTITUR FROM THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF AMENDED JUDGMENT AFTER REMITTITUR FROM THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT IS GRANTED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT SETS A STATUS CONFERENCE FOR 12/18/19 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER RE: THE PROPOSED FORM OF AMENDED JUDGMENT, WRIT OF MANDATE TO BE ENTERED BY THE COURT, AND ANY MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING OR DOCUMENTATION THAT MAY BE NECESSARY. PROPOSED FORM OF JUDGMENT, COMPETING ORDERS AND A BRIEF SUMMARY FROM EACH SIDE ARE TO BE SUMITTED BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON 12/16/19. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO MAY 21, 2014 TO GIVE RESPONDENTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. RESPONDENT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE "RETURN AND ANSWER" AND ALL OPPOSITION PAPERS FILED ON MARCH 11, 2014, RESPONDENT BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF HASTINGS COLLEGE OF LAW MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE "FIRST AMENDED RETURN" AND "NOTICE OF ERRATA" FILED ON MARCH 19, 2014, AND RESPONDENT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE "ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE" FILED ON MARCH 11, 2014 TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 15, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF ALL OTHER PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

Status Conference (Re: Proposed Form Of Amended Judgment, Writ Of Mandate, Mou - Per Hearing Held On 11/13/19)
Off Calendar Dec-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Peter Keith, Chief Attorney, 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Petitioner.
Benjamin Horwich, Esq. 415-512-4066 of Munger Tolles & Olson LLP for Respondents Regents Univeristy.
Robert Asperger, Deputy Attorney General, 916--327-7852 of the Office of the Attorney General for Respondent Board of Trustees Cal State University.
Benjamin Fay, Esq. 510-238-1400, Gabriel McWhirter, Esq. 510-238-1403 of Jarvis Fay & Gibson LLP for Respondent Board of Directors of UC Hastings.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Plaintiff informs the Court the City has reached an agreement with both the Regents and CSU regarding the implementation of the judgment on remand and remittitur from the California Supreme Court. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Status Conference (Re: Proposed Form Of Amended Judgment, Writ Of Mandate, Mou - Per Hearing Held On 11/13/19)

Judgment is signed. (see scanned judgment for details) Parties may come back on an ex parte basis to present the proposed judgment on Hastings and a proposed agreed upon writ. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

For Purposes Of Issuing Order Denying Complex Litigation
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Unknown

Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Amended Judgment After Remittitur From The California Supreme Court
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Peter Keith, Chief Attorney, 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Petitioner.
Benjamin Horwich, Esq. 415-512-4066 of Munger Tolles & Olson LLP for Respondents Regents Univeristy.
Robert Asperger, Deputy Attorney General, 916-210-7348 of the Office of the Attorney General for Respondent Board of Trustees Cal State University.
Benjamin Fay, Esq. 510-238-1400 of Jarvis Fay & Gibson LLP for Respondent Board of Directors of UC Hastings.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court discloses he has represented UC Regents, Hastings College of Law and maybe CSU while in private practice and is a graduate of Berkeley School of Law. However this will not affect the Courts ability to be fair and impartial. Respondent/Atty. Fay argues tax discrimination. Plaintiff responds re: non-discrimination, and cites cases.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Entry Of Amended Judgment After Remittitur From The California Supreme Court.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Entry Of Amended Judgment After Remittitur From The California Supreme Court is granted.

(see word minutes for full details)

In addition, the Court sets a status conference for 12/18/19 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Parties are ordered to meet and confer re: the proposed form of amended judgment, writ of mandate to be entered by the Court, and any memorandum of understanding or documentation that may be necessary. Proposed form of judgment, competing orders and a brief summary from each side are to be sumitted by the close of business on 12/16/19.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Apr-14-2014 Continued To May-21-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause Regarding Writ Of Mandate is continued to May 21, 2014 to give Respondents the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Respondent Regents of the University of California must deliver courtesy copies of the "Return and Answer" and all opposition papers filed on March 11, 2014, Respondent Board of Directors of Hastings College of Law must deliver courtesy copies of the "First Amended Return" and "Notice of Errata" filed on March 19, 2014, and Respondent The Board of Trustees of the California State University must deliver courtesy copies of the "Answer to Petition for Writ of Mandate" filed on March 11, 2014 to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 15, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the Order to Show Cause re: Peremptory Writ of Mandate will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of all other papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
